name: Google Cloud Natural Language API
what_it_does: Google Cloud NLP API for sentiment analysis, entity extraction, syntax analysis, content classification, and entity sentiment on text.
use_cases: Analyze customer feedback sentiment at scale to prioritize support queue routing
Extract structured entities (people, organizations, locations) from unstructured documents
Classify news articles or content into Google's taxonomy for content filtering pipelines
Build entity-linked knowledge graphs from text corpora using entity recognition with Wikipedia links
Perform multi-language sentiment and entity analysis without language detection preprocessing
not_for: Custom domain-specific entity recognition — use AutoML or Vertex AI for custom NLP models
Real-time streaming NLP at sub-100ms latency — this is a batch-oriented API
Applications requiring on-premise or offline NLP processing without cloud connectivity
best_when: Best for general-purpose NLP tasks (sentiment, entities, classification) without training custom models.
avoid_when: Avoid when domain-specific entity types or custom classification taxonomies are required.

auth.notes: Google Application Default Credentials or service account JSON key. Cloud NLP scope: cloud-language.googleapis.com.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5,000 units/month free per feature (sentiment, entity, syntax)
pricing.paid_tiers: $1-$2 per 1,000 units depending on feature; Healthcare NLP at premium pricing
pricing.notes: Units = 1,000 characters of text. Document classification billed per document.

performance.rate_limits: 600 requests/minute default; increase via quota request

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Google API error format with status, message, and details array — well-structured.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only analysis — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Text limit is 1,000,000 bytes per request — split documents larger than ~1MB before sending
Language detection is automatic but English-only models have higher accuracy — explicitly set language for non-English text
Entity sentiment is a separate feature from document sentiment and billed separately — don't conflate the two API calls
Knowledge Graph links (entity.metadata.wikipedia_url) require PROPER_NOUN entity type — common nouns don't get Wikipedia links
V1 and V2 (Healthcare NLP) are different products with different SDKs and endpoints — Healthcare NLP requires separate enablement

security_score_components.security_notes: Service account keys should use Workload Identity Federation where possible. Text content is not retained after processing.
